rent.note: furnished 1BR in Ciudad/Godoy Cruz; range $350–$600 USD; peso-pricing favors USD holders

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; budget $30–$80; hot summers need A/C but winters cold

groceries.note: home cooking; excellent local produce + wine at very low USD prices

diningOut.note: parrillas + local eateries; wine culture makes dining rich and affordable for USD holders

transit.note: buses $0.50–$1/trip; taxis cheap in USD; city bikeable

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; limited formal coworking; growing slowly

simData.note: Claro/Personal/Movistar 30-day; buy in pesos at favorable rate

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, winery visits, Andes hiking, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Southern hemisphere; harvest/wine season (Feb-Apr) can attract visitors.

evidence.claim: avg monthly cost ~$1,111–$1,300 for nomad; short-term at $1,300
evidence.quote: For a short-term stay (1-3 months), digital nomads can expect average monthly living costs of around $1,300 USD; Monthly costs land around $1,111 depending on your setup
One-bedroom apartments in Mendoza range from $350-600 monthly

evidence.claim: buses $0.50–$1/trip; Argentina peso devaluation benefits USD nomads
evidence.quote: Public transportation costs around $0.50-$1 USD per one-way ticket

evidence.claim: longer stays reduce costs; monthly budget $795 at minimum for single person
evidence.quote: If you stay longer in Mendoza, you can reduce your monthly living costs, mainly through longer-term rental contracts. A single person costs $795 per month
